comment: lab3 COMPLEX held-out benchmark (16 scenarios, 4 per sun rank). Lab2 + cross-zone spill + a shared Spotlight (Causes, +150 BOTH zones). Targets Z1=bright(3) AND Z2=bright(3). Physics: Z1 = 25 + (Z1Light?400) + (Z2Light?50) + (Z1Blinds?0.50*Sun) + (Z2Blinds?0.25*Sun) + (Spotlight?150); Z2 symmetric. Lamp ALWAYS reaches rank3 so every scenario is solvable. Spotlight (+150) is NEVER sufficient alone (needs >=300) so the optimum never uses it -> agent must learn to AVOID the redundant actuator. At sun=900 opening BOTH blinds gives 25+450+225=700 in each zone at ZERO energy = the cheap optimum. Sun in {0,100,400,900} via bounds [50,200,600].
description: sun=0, all off -> both lamps on (blinds/spotlight cannot reach rank3 at sun0)
sun=0, both at target via lamps -> hold
sun=0, Z1 rank1 (only Z2Light spill +50), Z2 at target -> turn Z1 lamp on
sun=0, both bright but spotlight ALSO on -> turn spotlight off (redundant energy)
sun=1, all off -> both lamps on
sun=1, Z1 at target, Z2 rank1 -> turn Z2 lamp on
sun=1, spotlight-only rank2 (175) -> insufficient: turn both lamps on (spotlight is redundant once lamps on)
sun=2, all off -> both lamps on (blind only +200 -> 225 rank2, insufficient)
sun=2, Z1 rank1, Z2 at target -> turn Z1 lamp on
sun=2, both bright but spotlight on -> turn spotlight off (redundant energy)

comment: === SUNSHINE RANK 3 (sun=900) — both blinds open = 700/zone at ZERO energy ===
description: sun=3, all off -> OPEN both blinds (700 each, zero energy) is the cheap optimum
sun=3, both bright via lamps (energy-costly) -> switch to blinds to save energy
sun=3, Z1 rank1, Z2 lamp on -> open both blinds (reaches Z1 and lets Z2 drop its lamp)
sun=3, both at target via blinds only -> hold (already zero-energy optimum)
